free_text: Photoexcitation of 76Ge
ELBE
Ee=7.8 MeV (700 uA) on Nb foil (7 um thick), or
Ee=12.3 MeV (700 uA) on Nb foil (12.5 um thick).
Low-energy part of the bremsstrahlung spectrum was
reduced by Al absorber (10 cm thick) behind the Nb
radiator.
1.8760 g enriched 76Ge, in a square shape of
(15 mm X 11 mm)
199.0 mg enriched 11B disk (20 mm in diam.) for
flux determination
Efficiency of ~100% relative to a NaI detector
(7.6 cm diam., 7.6 cm long).
Two HPGe detectors at 90 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 28 cm from the
target.
Two HPGe detectors at 127 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 32 cm from the
target.
Absorbers (8 mm Pb+3 mm Cu at 90 deg, 3 mm Pb+3 mm Cu
at 127 deg) were placed in front of HPGe detectors
to reduce the count rate due to low-energy photons.
All HPGe detectors were surrounded by BGO
scintillation detectors (3 cm thick).
Proof-read by Ronald Schwengner (2022-03-21)
VT
90 deg/127 deg intensity ratio
Below an excitation energy of 7.0 MeV the value was
deduced from the measurement at Ee = 7.8 MeV and for
higher excitation energies from the measurement at
Ee = 12.3 MeV.
The energy values was deduced from the gamma-ray
energy measured at 127 degree and corrected for
Doppler-shift
The following transitions are treated as inelastic
transitions and excluded:
- The sum of the energies of the transition and the
first or second 2+ state fits the energy of a
higher-lying transition within the uncertainties.
- The transitions observed up to 7.8 MeV in measurement
at max. Ee=12.3 MeV.
Spin assignment uncertain
Spin and parity assigned by S.Mukhopadhyay et al.
Spin and parity assigned by A.Jung et al.(1995)
Spin and parity by A.Jung et al.(1995), parity
assignment uncertain
source of the spin and parity
source of the spin and parity
No information on source of uncertainties.
Table I of Phys.Rev.C,105(2022)024303
Obtained by Ee=7.8 MeV irradiation
Obtained by Ee=12.3 MeV irradiation
Absorption (scattering) cross section was derived by
dividing the (g,g0) elastic scattering cross section
by the ground state transition branching ratio.
Selection of ground state transitions and estimation
of ground state transition branching ratios were done
by simulating gamma-ray cascades by the Monte Carlo
code DICEBOX with the level schemes obtained by the
code gammaDEX.
uncertainties including
- statistical uncertainties of the spectra
- uncertainties of the efficiencies and subtracted
simulated background
- spectra uncertainties of the flux resulting from the
integrated cross sections of the 11B levels
- uncertainties of the level-density parameters.
